inputs.label: Total Revenue
inputs.businessContext: Total revenue from auto shop services and parts over the analysis period. Based on standard financial reporting (GAAP).

inputs.label: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)
inputs.businessContext: Direct costs attributable to services and parts sold. Includes labor, parts, and overhead. Based on standard cost accounting.

inputs.label: Total Labor Cost
inputs.businessContext: Total wages, salaries, and benefits for technicians and service staff. Key driver of margin leakage if inefficient.

inputs.label: Parts Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of parts sold to customers. Includes markup leakage if not properly managed.

inputs.label: Warranty Claims
inputs.businessContext: Costs from warranty repairs and replacements. High values indicate quality issues or margin leakage.

inputs.label: Rework Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of redoing work due to errors. Directly impacts margin. Based on Six Sigma cost of poor quality.

inputs.label: Idle Time Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of technician idle time (non-billable). Indicates scheduling inefficiency. Based on Lean principles.

inputs.label: Inventory Holding Cost
inputs.businessContext: Cost of carrying parts inventory (storage, insurance, obsolescence). Based on APICS/SCOR standards.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: User's confidence in input data accuracy. Adjusts final output for uncertainty. Based on risk management practices.

inputs.label: Analysis Period (Days)
inputs.businessContext: Duration of analysis period. Used for annualizing costs. Standard financial period.

thresholds.grossMarginPercent < 0.4: Gross margin below 40% indicates significant leakage.
thresholds.laborCostPercent > 0.35: Labor cost ratio above 35% suggests inefficiency.
thresholds.warrantyPercent > 0.05: Warranty claims above 5% of revenue indicate quality issues.
thresholds.reworkPercent > 0.1: Rework cost above 10% of labor cost indicates process problems.
thresholds.idleTimePercent > 0.1: Idle time above 10% of labor cost indicates scheduling issues.
thresholds.inventoryHoldingPercent > 0.2: Inventory holding cost above 20% of parts cost suggests overstocking.

suggestedActions: If gross margin low: Review pricing strategy and cost control.
If labor cost high: Implement lean scheduling and cross-training.
If warranty claims high: Improve quality control and supplier management.
If rework high: Standardize processes and invest in training.
If idle time high: Optimize appointment scheduling and reduce bottlenecks.
If inventory holding high: Implement just-in-time inventory and reduce stock levels.
